articles.title: Delhi Crime Alert: 17-Year-Old Boy Stabbed to Death in New Usmanpur
articles.description: A shocking murder case has been reported from the New Usmanpur area of Delhi, where a 17-year-old boy was allegedly stabbed to death. According to initial reports, police rushed to the scene after receiving information about the attack. Upon arrival, officers found the minor lying on the ground with severe injuries. He had already succumbed to his wounds. Police have registered a case and launched an investigation into the incident. Several teams have been formed to identify and arrest those responsible for the killing. A large police presence was seen in the area following the crime, indicating the seriousness of the case. Local residents expressed concern over the incident, especially as it reportedly occurred during the daytime when markets were open and people were moving around the locality. Some witnesses claimed that a group of around 12 to 15 youths may have been involved in the attack. The incident has raised fresh concerns about public safety and law and order in the national capital.

articles.title: Bail is rule, jail an exception, but not absolute: Supreme Court
articles.description: Bail rules: The Supreme Court said bail remains the rule and jail the exception, but courts must weigh facts, evidence, offence severity and law. The ruling came in a Jammu murder case, granting relief to two elderly accused while denying others.

The season promises significant map changes and regular content updates.

The company's approach to bitcoin accumulation remains its core strategy.

articles.description: A dormant Obama-era White House Instagram account suddenly became the center of a bizarre hacking mystery after strange AI-generated posts and controversial messages appeared online. The official @obamawhitehouse account, inactive since President Donald Trump's first inauguration in 2017, was reportedly compromised before Meta stepped in to secure it. Who was behind the cyber attack, and why target an account that had been silent for years? Here's everything we know.

articles.description: Launched to counter the West Asia crisis impact, ECLGS 5.0 has seen huge demand, with 2.62 lakh applications worth Rs 1.71 lakh crore. Sanctions total Rs 35,194 crore, with MSMEs being the primary beneficiaries of the credit scheme.

articles.title: India repatriates 2,411 from Myanmar scams, 150+ still trapped
articles.description: The MEA confirmed that 2,411 Indians trapped in cyber scam compounds in Myanmar have been repatriated over the last 1.5 years. However, over 150 citizens are still believed to be stuck, with India working with Myanmar for their return.

articles.title: DSSSB Advt 03/2026 released for 1,979 Group B and C vacancies, applications begin June 16
articles.description: The Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released Advertisement No. 03/2026 announcing 1,979 vacancies for various Group B and Group C posts across Delhi government departments, autonomous bodies and local bodies. The online application process will begin on June 16, 2026, and continue until July 15, 2026. Vacancies are available for teaching, scientific, technical and administrative positions. Selection will involve prescribed recruitment procedures, including examinations and verification stages.

articles.title: Inflation Blow: LPG Rates Increased in Delhi and Kolkata
articles.description: Consumers have received another inflation-related setback as the prices of commercial LPG cylinders have been increased in several cities across India. In Delhi, the price of a 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder has been raised by ₹42, taking the new rate to ₹3,113.50. Similarly, in Kolkata, the price has increased by ₹53.50, bringing the cost of a 19-kg commercial cylinder to ₹3,255.50. The price hike is expected to impact businesses that rely heavily on commercial LPG, including restaurants, hotels, and small enterprises. In addition, the cost of 5-kg free-trade LPG cylinders has also been increased by ₹11. Following the revision, the new price of a 5-kg free-trade LPG cylinder in Delhi stands at ₹821.50. The revised rates have come into effect immediately from today. However, there is some relief for households, as the prices of domestic LPG cylinders remain unchanged for now. The latest increase adds to concerns over rising costs and inflationary pressures affecting consumers and businesses alike.
